data.title: Perplexity adds to publisher program as gen-AI requires quality content
data.description:
Perplexity, an artificial intelligence-powered search engine, backed by the likes of Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN) founder Jeff Bezos, continues to add names to its publisher program.

Today, the startup announced the addition of more than a dozen new media partners, including ADWEEK, Blavity, DPReview, Gear Patrol, The Independent, Lee Enterprises, Los Angeles Times, MediaLab, Mexico News Daily Minkabu Infonoid, NewsPicks, Prisa Media, RTL Germany brands stern and ntv and World History Encyclopedia.

The media companies receive a slice of revenue generated from advertising.

"We would not be able to serve factual, valuable answers without news organizations continuing to report on different topics," said Jessica Chan, Perplexity's new Head of Publisher Partnerships. "We're excited to welcome these new publishers to the program, representing over 25 countries across four continents."

Chan previously built LinkedIn's content partner program.

Perplexity already partners with Time, Der Spiegel, Fortune, Entrepreneur, The Texas Tribune and WordPress.com. Perplexity is also working with Northwestern University's Medill School of Journalism, Media, Integrated Marketing Communications, to research the potential benefits of responsible AI in the newsroom.

However, the AI startup also faces a copyright infringement lawsuit by the News Corp-owned (NWS)(NWSA) Wall Street Journal and New York Post filed in October.

data.title: Toast cut to Neutral at D.A. Davidson on CFO's margin comment
data.description:
D.A. Davidson downgraded Toast (NYSE:TOST) to Neutral from Buy after the company's chief financial officer unexpectedly lowered expectations for 2025 adjusted EBITDA margins at an investment conference.

CFO Elena Gomez indicated 100-200 basis points of expansion for adjusted EBITDA as a percentage of fintech and subscription gross profit, compared with consensus embedding 360 bps of expansion, analyst Peter Heckmann said on Thursday.

"This surprise margin commentary was unsettling, and the valuation doesn’t leave much room for uncertainty," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Still, D.A. Davidson "believes management is making the right moves to make Toast (NYSE:TOST) a long-term leader in the integrated software and payment solutions market for the restaurant (and retail) verticals."

Management didn't provide material additional comments during the conference, but Heckmann inferred that Toast (TOST) wants to boost investments in sales & marketing and R&D to reach its goal of increasing recurring gross profits at 20% or more for the next several years.

As a result of the comment, the analyst is reducing his adjusted EBITDA forecasts for 2025 and 2026 by 9%-10% each.

D.A. Davidson's Neutral rating on the stock agrees with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

Toast (TOST) stock was unfazed, edging down 0.2% in early afternoon trading on Thursday.

data.title: Relmada cut to neutral by Mizuho on Phase 3 study failure
data.description:
Mizuho has downgraded Relmada Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RLMD) to neutral on the apparent demise of its drug candidate REL-1017 for major depressive disorder.

The investment bank said it was disappointed that a Data Monitoring Committee determined a Phase 3 study for the drug was unlikely to meet its primary endpoint, adding that the determination “likely means the end of the line for REL-1017 in MDD.”

As a result, Mizuho removed its sales forecast for the drug from its model and cut its price target to $1 from $23.

data.title: JinkoSolar jumps as Jefferies reiterates Buy, raises price target to Street-high $65
data.description:
JinkoSolar (NYSE:JKS) +1.5% in Thursday's trading as Jefferies maintains its Buy rating while raising its stock price target to a Street-high $65 from $36, saying the top global solar module maker is "deeply undervalued" with a $1.2B market cap.

We may be entering a new era for solar, Jefferies says, with JinkoSolar (JKS) "as the lead coordinator [which] may benefit the most from the mechanism that determines production plan by weighting on historical market share [and] nominal capacity... with its global #1 shipment volume in 2023 and H1 2024 and leading amount of N-type TOPCon capacities."

Jefferies notes awareness by overseas investors on supply self-discipline has been low, expecting that "as overseas investors understand and appreciate the seriousness and granularity of the supply-side reform, JKS will see significant room for re-rating.

data.title: LendingTree falls amid Culper Research short report
data.description:
• LendingTree (NASDAQ:TREE) quickly dropped 9% amid a short report from Culper Research.
  • Culper Research disclosed that it's short LendingTree (TREE).
  • LendingTree didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha email request for comment.
  • LendingTree (TREE) has short interest of 6.4%.

data.title: Pfizer, Lilly CEOs have dinner at Mar-a-Lago with Trump, RFK Jr. - report
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) CEO Albert Bourla and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) CEO Dave Ricks dined with President-elect Donald Trump and Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., his nominee to run HHS, at Mar-a-Lago the evening of Dec. 4.
  • A report from Axios noted that Trump "discussed how the public and private sectors can collaborate on finding cures for cancer" and other issues.
  • Axios also said Steve Ubl, CEO of the Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA), the major drug industry lobbying group, also joined.
  • The meeting could provide a sigh of relief for the pharma and biotech industries who were initially worried about Trump's pick of RFK Jr. to lead HHS given his hesitancy about vaccines. RFK Jr. has also been highly critical of the FDA.

data.title: Microsoft, Salesforce look to benefit as IT budgets grow in 2025: survey
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and other enterprise software vendors should benefit in 2025 as a survey of chief information officers by KeyBanc shows IT budgets are slated to increase in 2025.

Budgets are expected to grow 3.9% year over year in 2025, the survey found. Of the large companies surveyed, 71% of respondents expect IT budget increases in 2025, and 82% of small businesses surveyed expect increases as well.

"We are incrementally more positive following our second half 2024 survey than during our midyear report six months ago," said KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader, in a detailed report. "We see positive signs of a potential rebound within front office applications in 2025 with budget priority expectations for the year increasing."

"Specific vendor callouts, include CRM increasing in priority and MSFT remaining a top cloud vendor and Generative AI beneficiary, a category that saw expectations for spend increase this survey," he added.

Salesforce remains one of the most entrenched enterprise software names, according to the survey. Meanwhile, Microsoft continued to make gains as a cloud provider as more and more businesses move data into the cloud.

Regarding cybersecurity and analytics, Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW), Crowdstrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW) and MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB) received strong survey results.

Snowflake ranked as the top strategic, independent data and analytics vendor by respondents. CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks were both highly ranked security vendors, with CrowdStrike seeing no customers who were surveyed migrating following the summer outage.

However, despite the likely uptick in IT spending in 2025, 62% of respondents said budget scrutiny is increasing as well.

Regarding the recent U.S. presidential election, the vast majority, or 88%, said the outcome had no negative impact on IT budgets.

When it came to pricing, Microsoft, Oracle (ORCL) and Salesforce were named as the most aggressive vendors regarding price increases.

data.title: Comprehensive Healthcare Systems launches $1.24M securities offering
data.description:
Comprehensive Healthcare Systems (CHS:CA) has launched a private offering of 124M units to raise gross proceeds of up to $1.24M.

Each unit consists of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant, and is offered at $0.01 per unit.

Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one share at a price of $0.05 each for a period of 36 months from the closing date of the offering.

Proceeds of the offering will be used for working capital and for meeting certain essential obligations of the company. None of the proceeds received will be used to pay non-arm's length parties and the company confirms that not more than 10% of the proceeds of the proposed financing will be used for investor relations activities.

data.title: GE Vernova on the rise after new bullish notes from TD Cowen, Guggenheim
data.description:
GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) +2.6% in Thursday's trading as TD Cowen's Marc Bianchi adds his name to the long list of analysts with bullish takes on the company, initiating coverage with a Buy rating and $400 price target.

GE Vernova (GEV) "seems well positioned to benefit from an inflection of investment in electricity supply," Bianchi writes, seeing margin expansion driving upside to consensus in 2026 and beyond, pointing to the company's December 10 investor update as a potential catalyst for positive revisions.

Bianchi forecasts GE Vernova's (GEV) FY 2025 EBITDA margins at ~12% in 2026, roughly double the 6% level expected for this year.

Guggenheim analyst Joseph Osha reiterates his Buy rating and raises his price target to $400 from $300, seeing Wall Street's cash flow generation consensus estimates as "unaccountably low," and expecting return of capital to feature prominently in next week's update.

Earlier this week, Wolfe Research initiated coverage with an Outperform rating and a $403 price target.

data.title: Insulet awarded $452M in damages in EOFlow case
data.description:
• Insulet (NASDAQ:PODD) said a federal jury has awarded the company $452M in damages in its case against South Korea's EOFlow.
  • The jury found EOFlow and several other defendants misappropriated Insulet’s (PODD) trade secrets. It awarded Insulet (PODD) $170M in compensatory damages and an additional $282M in exemplary damages, according to a statement.
  • Insulet (PODD) added it was uncertain if EOFlow would be able to satisfy the damage award.
